notes: RISC-V core, ultra-low power, low price. Only 1 hardware I2C; multiple I2C devices require software I2C (bitbang)

onboard_peripherals.name: LED
onboard_peripherals.notes: Onboard indicator LED. Most projects don't need to control it; GPIO8 can be freed

notes: All I2C/SPI/UART can be remapped via GPIO Matrix (simpler than ESP32, no input_only pins)
GPIO 2/8/9 are strapping pins; their level at startup affects the operating mode
GPIO 8 is also the onboard LED; caution needed when used as strapping
GPIO 12-17 are occupied by internal Flash and cannot be used externally
GPIO 18/19 are USB serial pins; cannot be repurposed when using USB serial

Only 1 hardware I2C controller. When connecting multiple I2C devices, they can share the same bus if addresses don't conflict; if addresses conflict, use machine.SoftI2C to emulate on other GPIOs (any free GPIO works)
RISC-V core; some MicroPython libraries may be less mature than for Xtensa (ESP32)
